- [mricom-5-4-ocean-biogeochemistry](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_component.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Component%3A+mricom-5-4-ocean-biogeochemistry&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5281%2Fzenodo.20839616+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-10-2169-2017+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2007GB002953+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2009GB003577+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2025MS005395+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.2467%2Fmripapers.64.1&component=%22ocean-biogeochemistry%22&family=%22%22&description=The+ocean+biogeochemistry+component+of+MRI-ESM3.4+%28Deushi+et+al.%2C+2026%29+simulates+the+carbon+cycle+using+formulations+for+air%E2%80%93sea+gas+exchange+and+carbonate+chemistry+following+the+OMIP+CMIP6+protocol+%28Orr+et+al.%2C+2017%29+together+with+biological+processes+represented+by+an+NPZD-type+marine+ecosystem+model+based+on+Schmittner+et+al.+%282008%2C+2009%29.+A+key+update+is+the+revision+of+biological+production+rates%2C+whereby+the+previous+implicit+representation+of+micronutrient+limitation%E2%80%94through+a+globally+low+maximum+production+rate%E2%80%94has+been+replaced+by+an+approach+that+increases+this+rate+and+explicitly+accounts+for+iron+limitation+based+on+dissolved+iron+concentrations+%28Tsujino+et+al.%2C+2026%29%2C+compared+with+the+previous+versions+described+by+Adachi+et+al.+%282013%29.&code_base=)

- [mricom-5-4-sea-ice](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_component.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Component%3A+mricom-5-4-sea-ice&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5281%2Fzenodo.20839616+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.11483%2Fmritechrepo.87+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2FJC094iC08p10937+https%3A%2F%2Fcsdms.colorado.edu%2Fw%2Fimages%2FCICE_documentation_and_software_user%27s_manual.pdf+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.ocemod.2023.102288+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2020MS002103+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.2151%2Fjmsj.2019-051&component=%22sea_ice%22&family=%22%22&description=The+sea-ice+component+of+MRI-ESM3.4+%28Deushi+et+al.%2C+2026%29+uses+an+OGCM+developed+at+the+Meteorological+Research+Institute+%28MRI%29+named+MRI.COM+version+5+%28Sakamoto+et+al.%2C+2023%29.+It+is+based+on+the+model+of+Mellor+and+Kantha+%281989%29+and+incorporates+some+additional+processes%2Fschemes%2C+such+as+categorization+by+thickness%2C+ridging+and+rheology%2C+from+the+Los+Alamos+sea+ice+model+%28CICE%29+%28Hunke+and+Lipscomb%2C+2010%29.+The+number+of+sea-ice+categories+is+8+with+their+upper+boundaries+of+0.1%2C+0.3%2C+0.6%2C+1.4%2C+2.4%2C+3.6%2C+5.0+and+30+m%2C+respectively.+The+following+new+processes%2Fschemes+are+incorporated+in+the+current+version.+Following+Toyoda+et+al.+%282024%29%2C+the+model+takes+into+account+variable+sea-ice+salinity+and+revises+the+snow+ice+formation+process.+It+also+adopts+the+grease+ice+scheme+of+Mackie+et+al.+%282020%29.+These+updates+represent+improvements+compared+with+MRI-ESM2.0+%28Yukimoto+et+al.%2C+2019%29.&code_base=)

- [mricom-5-4](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_component.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Component%3A+mricom-5-4&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5281%2Fzenodo.20839616+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.11483%2Fmritechrepo.87+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.ocemod.2024.102495+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2FJD091iD06p06671+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2F0021-9991%2884%2990143-8+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1175%2F1520-0485%281995%29025%253C0463%3APEITTI%253E2.0.CO%3B2+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2019MS001992+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.ocemod.2010.09.002+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs10872-020-00584-0+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2023MS004106+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.2151%2Fjmsj.2019-051&component=%22ocean%22&family=%22%22&description=The+ocean+component+of+MRI-ESM3.4+%28Deushi+et+al.%2C+2026%29+uses+an+OGCM+developed+at+the+Meteorological+Research+Institute+%28MRI%29+named+MRI.COM+version+5+%28Sakamoto+et+al.%2C+2023%29.+It+solves+the+primitive+equations+under+the+Boussinesq+approximation.+This+model+uses+a+mixed-precision+version+%28Nakano+et+al.%2C+2025%29+of+the+SOM+scheme+%28Prather%2C+1986%29+for+active+tracers+and+the+PPM+scheme+%28Colella+and+Woodward%2C+1984%29+for+passive+tracers.+It+updates+parameterizations+of+neutral+physics+%28Gent+et+al.%2C+1995%3B+Shao+et+al.%2C+2020%29%2C+mixed+layer+eddies+%28Fox-Kemper+et+al.%2C+2011%29%2C+tide-induced+vertical+mixing+%28Kawasaki+et+al.%2C+2021%29%2C+and+sub-grid+scale+convection+%28G%C3%BClk+et+al.%2C+2024%29%2C+compared+with+MRI-ESM2.0+%28Yukimoto+et+al.%2C+2019%29.&code_base=)

- [nemo-gosi9](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_component.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Component%3A+nemo-gosi9&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-18-377-2025&component=%22ocean%22&family=%22nemo%22&description=This+is+the+UK+physical+global+ocean+configuration+of+the+NEMO+%28Nucleus+for+European+Modelling+of+the+Ocean%29+model.+Updates+to+the+ocean+configuration+GOSI9+compared+to+the+GO6+ocean+used+in+CMIP6+include+the+use+of+the+NEMO+v4.0+code+base%2C+a+change+in+the+sea+ice+model+to+SI3%2C+use+of+the+TEOS-10+equation+of+state%2C+improvements+to+the+time+stepping+and+numerical+mixing+schemes%2C+tuning+of+southern+ocean+performance+in+higher+resolution+models+and+an+update+to+the+iceberg+climatology+used.+For+a+full+description+of+changes+see+Guiavarc%27h+et+al%2C+2025.&code_base=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5281%2Fzenodo.13814369)

- [nemo_v3_6](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_component.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Component%3A+nemo_v3_6&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&name=NEMO+v3.6&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2019MS001791&component=%22ocean%22&family=%22nemo%22&description=Nucleus+for+European+Modelling+of+the+Ocean+version+3.6+%28OPA%29.&code_base=private)

- [openifs-48r1](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_component.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Component%3A+openifs-48r1&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.21957%2Fslk503fs2i+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1175%2FJAS-D-13-0163.1+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1002%2Fqj.713+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1175%2F2008JHM1068.1+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2019MS001725+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1175%2FMWR-D-18-0043.1+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1256%2Fqj.03.173+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.21957%2F02054f0fbf&component=%22atmosphere%22&family=%22ifs%22&description=%2A%2AMain+processes+simulated%2A%2A+OpenIFS+48r1+simulates+the+global+three-dimensional+atmospheric+circulation+together+with+its+interactions+with+the+land+surface%2C+sea+ice+and+ocean+wave+fields.+The+model+integrates+the+hydrostatic+primitive+equations+on+a+terrain-following+mass-based+vertical+coordinate%2C+prognosing+wind%2C+temperature%2C+specific+humidity%2C+cloud+condensate+%28liquid%2C+ice%29%2C+precipitation+species+%28rain%2C+snow%29%2C+cloud+fraction+and+surface+pressure.+Coupled+subsystems+include+the+HTESSEL+land-surface+scheme+%28soil+moisture+and+temperature%2C+vegetation%2C+snow%2C+lakes+via+FLake%29+and+the+WAM+ocean+wave+model.+New+at+this+cycle%2C+OpenIFS+48r1+includes+interactive+prognostic+ozone+and+consolidates+OpenIFS+and+OpenIFS%2FAC+into+a+single+code+base%2C+with+the+operational+CAMS+chemistry+and+aerosol+package+available+by+default.++%2A%2AKey+physics+and+parameterizations%2A%2A+Cycle+48r1+represents+a+major+upgrade+of+the+moist+physics+package%2C+restructuring+the+call+sequence+of+the+turbulence%2C+shallow+and+deep+convection%2C+prognostic+cloud+and+microphysics+schemes+for+greater+physical+consistency.+Notable+changes+include+a+new+deep-convection+closure+with+additional+dependence+on+total+advective+moisture+convergence%3B+a+change+from+exponential%E2%80%93exponential+to+random%E2%80%93exponential+cloud+vertical+overlap%3B+a+vapour-deposition+process+for+falling+ice%3B+and+revised+microphysics+permitting+supercooled+drizzle+drops+to+form+and+only+freeze+on+contact+with+ice%2Fsnow+or+at+much+colder+temperatures%2C+enabling+the+prediction+of+freezing-drizzle+events.+Saturation+adjustment+and+ice+fall+speed+have+been+revised%2C+and+saturation-tendency+perturbations+have+been+removed+from+SPPT+to+suppress+rare+instabilities%2C+while+supersaturation+adjustment+has+been+added+to+ensemble+SPPT+perturbations+with+mass-weighting+and+a+relaxation+timescale.+Radiation+uses+the+ecRad+scheme.+The+HTESSEL+land+surface+is+upgraded+from+a+single-layer+to+a+multi-layer+snow+scheme%2C+markedly+improving+snowpack+realism+and+the+diurnal+cycle+of+2+m+temperature+in+snow-covered+regions.+The+dynamical+core+gains+a+new+vertical+finite-element+discretisation%2C+global+mass+fixers+active+for+all+moist+species+including+water+vapour%2C+a+revised+semi-Lagrangian+iterative+trajectory+solver%2C+and+cubic+semi-Lagrangian+departure-point+interpolation+with+a+3-D+quasi-monotone+limiter+for+cloud+and+precipitation+variables.++%2A%2ANotable+features+of+this+version%2A%2A+Relative+to+the+previous+release+%28OpenIFS+43r3v2%29%2C+48r1+brings%3A+%28i%29+the+major+moist+physics+restructuring%3B+%28ii%29+the+multi-layer+snow+scheme%3B+%28iii%29+interactive+prognostic+ozone%3B+%28iv%29+prediction+of+freezing+drizzle%3B+%28v%29+improved+water+and+energy+conservation+in+the+dynamics%3B+%28vi%29+consolidation+of+OpenIFS+and+OpenIFS%2FAC+into+a+single+code+base+with+operational+CAMS+chemistry+and+aerosol+available+by+default%3B+%28vii%29+a+revised+SPPT+formulation%3B+and+%28viii%29+updated+climate+fields+%28orography%2C+land%E2%80%93sea+mask%2C+lake+depth%2C+glacier+mask%29.+Technical+upgrades+include+migration+from+FCM+to+ecbuild%2Fcmake%2C+a+major+code+restructure%2C+single-+and%2For+double-precision+builds+as+default%2C+inclusion+of+the+Single+Column+Model+%28SCM%29+built+alongside+the+3-D+model%2C+and+the+ifs-test+framework+for+known-good-output+regression+testing.&code_base=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fecmwf-ifs%2Fopenifs)

- [piscesv2-gas](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_component.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Component%3A+piscesv2-gas&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&name=PISCESv2-gas&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2019MS001791%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fbg-18-3823-2021%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fesd-14-399-2023&component=%22ocean-biogeochemistry%22&family=%22pisces%22&description=Pelagic+Interaction+Scheme+for+Carbon+and+Ecosystem+Studies+model+volume+2+version+trace+gases.&code_base=https%3A%2F%2Fgitlab.in2p3.fr%2Fpisco%2Fpisces-gas%2Fpisces-gas)

- [pism-v1-2-line](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_component.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Component%3A+pism-v1-2-line&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fagupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com%2Fdoi%2F10.1029%2F2008JF001179+https%3A%2F%2Ftc.copernicus.org%2Farticles%2F5%2F715%2F2011%2F+https%3A%2F%2Fzenodo.org%2Frecords%2F19443904&component=%22land-ice%22&family=%22PISM%22&description=The+Parallel+Ice+Sheet+Model+%28PISM%29+is+an+open+source%2C+parallel%2C+high-resolution+land+ice+%28i.e.+ice+sheet%29+model%2C+principally+driven+by+gravity+and+the+surface+mass+balance.+Gravity+is+partly+balanced+by+lateral+and+bottom+friction%2C+the+resulting+shear+stress+balance+in+the+ice+body+%28which+depends+on+the+ice+temperature+field+via+a+polythermal%2C+enthalpy-based+energy-conserving+scheme%29+determines+the+ice+flow.+PISM+includes+marine+ice+sheet+physics+with+dynamic+calving+fronts.++Here+the+PISM+v1.2-line+is+used+which+is+slightly+modified+by+DMI+in+the+context+of+coupling+PISM+with+the+ESM+EC-Earth3-ESM-1-1.+I.+e.+PISM+is+forced+via+the+climatological+fields+coming+from+EC-Earth3-ESM-1-1+which+are+down-scaled+to+a+mass-balance+over+the+Greenland+ice+sheet++forcing+PISMs+ice+mechanics.+PISM+provides+the+fresh+water+input+for+the+ocean+and+updates+of+the+Greenland+surface+topography+for+the+atmosphere+component+of+EC-Earth3-ESM-1-1.+The+albedo+is+determined+by+the+interaction+of+the+components.&code_base=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fpism%2Fpism)

- [reprobus-c_v2_0](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_component.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Component%3A+reprobus-c_v2_0&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&name=REPROBUS-C+%28v2.0%29&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2019MS001816&component=%22atmospheric-chemistry%22&family=%22reprobus%22&description=The+chemistry+scheme+of+CNRM-ESM2+is+an+on-line+scheme+whereby+the+chemistry+routines+are+part+of+the+physics+of+the+atmospheric+climate+model.&code_base=private)

- [wombatlite](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_component.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Component%3A+wombatlite&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fbg-22-5349-2025&component=%22ocean-biogeochemistry%22&family=%22wombat%22&description=The+World+Ocean+Model+of+Biogeochemistry+And+Trophic-dynamics+%28WOMBAT%29+is+based+on+a+NPZD+%28nutrient%E2%80%93phytoplankton%E2%80%93zooplankton%E2%80%93detritus%29+model.+The+%22lite%22+version+of+WOMBAT+includes+one+class+each+of+phytoplankton%2C+zooplankton+and+sinking+detritus%2C+as+well+as+nitrate+%28NO3%29%2C+bio-available+iron+%28Fe%29%2C+dissolved+inorganic+carbon+%28DIC%29%2C+calcium+carbonate+%28CaCO3%29%2C+alkalinity+%28ALK%29%2C+and+oxygen+%28O2%29.+Fe+is+carried+through+the+zooplankton+and+detrital+pools+as+well.+Gas+exchange+follows+OCMIP2+protocols.&code_base=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5281%2Fzenodo.17180330)

- [CAS-ESM](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+CAS-ESM&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22atmosphere%22%2C%22land-surface%22%2C%22ocean%22%2C%22sea_ice%22%2C%22ocean-biogeochemistry%22%2C%22atmospheric-chemistry%22%2C%22aerosol%22%2C%22land-ice%22&representative_member=CAS-ESM1.0%2C+CAS-ESM2.0&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2020MS002210%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.11871%2Fjfdc.issn.2096-742X.2020.01.004%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs13351-025-4915-6&website=&primary_institution=%22cas%22&description=The+Chinese+Academy+of+Sciences+Earth+System+Model+%28CAS-ESM%29+family+is+an+independently+developed+Earth+system+modeling+framework+designed+to+simulate+the+coupled+evolution+of+the+climate+system+and+related+biogeochemical+processes.+It+provides+a+flexible+numerical+platform+for+representing+interactions+among+major+Earth+system+components%2C+including+the+atmosphere%2C+ocean%2C+land+surface%2C+sea+ice%2C+aerosols%2C+atmospheric+chemistry%2C+terrestrial+ecosystems%2C+fire+processes%2C+ocean+biogeochemistry%2C+and+other+relevant+processes.+The+CAS-ESM+family+supports+a+wide+range+of+applications%2C+including+historical+climate+simulation%2C+future+climate+projection%2C+climate+variability+and+predictability+studies%2C+carbon%E2%80%93climate+feedback+analysis%2C+and+regional+climate+downscaling.+Different+members+of+the+family+are+configured+with+varying+levels+of+component+complexity%2C+spatial+and+vertical+resolutions%2C+model+domains+or+model+tops%2C+process+representations%2C+and+parameter+settings.+These+configurations+enable+CAS-ESM+to+address+scientific+questions+across+multiple+temporal+and+spatial+scales%2C+from+regional+climate+processes+to+global+Earth+system+change.&collaborative_institutions=%22bnu%22%2C%22sysu%22&established=1986&family_type=%22model%22)

[CoLM](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+CoLM&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22land-surface%22&representative_member=CoLM2024&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1175%2FBAMS-84-8-1013%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-18-6517-2025%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2025GL116015%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1038%2Fs41597-025-04729-y%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs00376-024-3338-0%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2024JD041520%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2024JD041338%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fessd-17-517-2025%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2023GL107059%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fessd-17-117-2025%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2022MS003567%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.3390%2Frs15071780%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2022GL101754%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2021WR031589%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.3390%2Ff12060722%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2019MS001723%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2019WR025368%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2019MS001784%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.agrformet.2019.02.006&website=&primary_institution=%22sysu%22&description=The+Common+Land+Model+%28CoLM%29+is+a+comprehensive+land+surface+modeling+system+designed+to+quantify+exchanges+of+energy%2C+water%2C+momentum%2C+carbon%2C+nutrients%2C+and+human-induced+disturbances+between+the+land+surface+and+the+atmosphere.+Its+scientific+focus+has+evolved+from+representing+basic+soil%E2%80%93vegetation%E2%80%93atmosphere+interactions+to+an+integrated+Earth-system+framework+that+supports+high-resolution+simulations%2C+process+diagnosis%2C+climate+and+hydrological+applications%2C+and+assessment+of+ecological+and+anthropogenic+impacts.+CoLM+emphasizes+physically+based+parameterizations%2C+multi-source+observations%2C+high-resolution+datasets%2C+scale+transformation%2C+and+coupling+with+weather%2C+climate%2C+and+Earth+system+models.++A+central+feature+of+CoLM+is+its+common+code+base+and+shared+physical+framework.+The+model+was+originally+developed+to+combine+robust+components+from+major+land+models+while+reducing+duplicated+efforts+across+the+land-modeling+community.+Its+modular+structure+allows+common+treatments+of+canopy+radiation%2C+turbulence%2C+soil+heat+and+water+transport%2C+snow+and+frozen+soil%2C+runoff%2C+lakes%2C+biogeochemistry%2C+and+land-cover+heterogeneity%2C+while+enabling+users+to+test+alternative+schemes+and+add+new+processes+within+a+unified+platform.++The+major+CoLM+members+differ+in+both+process+complexity+and+system+capability.+CoLM2004+established+the+Chinese+Common+Land+Model+based+on+the+prototype+CLM%2C+with+improved+global+land-surface+datasets%2C+a+two-big-leaf+canopy+model%2C+a+slab-ocean+option%2C+reorganized+code+structure%2C+and+parallel+computation.+CoLM2014+further+improved+land-cover%2C+soil%2C+and+LAI+datasets%2C+introduced+an+enhanced+lake+model%2C+coupled+the+CaMa-Flood+river-routing+model%2C+and+made+the+system+more+user-friendly.+CoLM2024+represents+a+major+expansion+into+a+complete+land+simulation+platform%2C+adding+unstructured+and+watershed-based+grids%2C+PFT+and+plant-community+subgrid+structures%2C+updated+hydrology%2C+biogeochemistry%2C+urban%2C+crop%2C+reservoir%2C+fire%2C+ozone-stress%2C+and+downscaling+modules%2C+together+with+more+flexible+data+handling+and+high-performance+computing+capability.&collaborative_institutions=%22bnu%22&established=2005&family_type=%22component%22)

- [IAP-AGCM](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+IAP-AGCM&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22atmosphere%22%2C%22atmospheric-chemistry%22%2C%22aerosol%22&representative_member=IAP-AGCM1%2C+IAP-AGCM2%2C+IAP-AGCM3%2C+IAP-AGCM4.1%2C+IAP-AGCM5.0&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2FBF02919169%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2FBF02661533%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2FBF02656847%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.3878%2Fj.issn.1006-9895.2009.06.13%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1175%2FMWR-D-11-00367.1%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.aosl.2020.100025%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2020MS002210&website=&primary_institution=%22cas%22&description=The+IAP+Atmospheric+General+Circulation+Model+%28IAP-AGCM%29+family+is+a+family+of+global+atmospheric+models+developed+at+the+Institute+of+Atmospheric+Physics%2C+Chinese+Academy+of+Sciences%2C+for+simulating+atmospheric+circulation%2C+climate+variability%2C+and+key+atmospheric+physical+processes.+The+family+is+characterized+by+a+shared+grid-point+dynamical+framework%2C+finite-difference+numerical+formulation%2C+terrain-following+vertical+coordinate%2C+and+a+common+set+of+atmospheric+physics+schemes.+The+scientific+focus+of+IAP-AGCM+includes+large-scale+circulation%2C+boundary-layer+processes%2C+convection%2C+clouds%2C+radiation%2C+gravity-wave+effects%2C+and+atmosphere%E2%80%93surface+interactions.+Family+members+differ+mainly+in+horizontal+and+vertical+resolution%2C+model+top%2C+physical+parameterization+options%2C+coupling+configuration%2C+and+parameter+tuning.+These+differences+allow+IAP-AGCM+to+support+both+standalone+atmospheric+simulations+and+its+use+as+the+atmospheric+component+of+coupled+climate+and+Earth+system+models.&collaborative_institutions=%22%22&established=1983&family_type=%22component%22)

- [LICOM](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+LICOM&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22ocean%22&representative_member=LICOM3&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs00376-019-9208-5%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs13351-012-0305-y%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.ocemod.2022.102063%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs00376-020-0150-3%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs00376-020-0057-z&website=https%3A%2F%2Fearthlab-data.iap.ac.cn%2FLFS%2F&primary_institution=%22cas+the+institute+of+atmospheric+physics+%28iap%29%2C+chinese+academy+of+sciences+%28cas%29%22&description=The+LICOM+ocean+general+circulation+model+is+a+global+ocean+circulation+model+independently+developed+by+the+Ocean+Modelling+Group+at+the+Institute+of+Atmospheric+Physics+%28IAP%29%2C+Chinese+Academy+of+Sciences+%28CAS%29.+It+aims+to+provide+a+numerical+ocean+model+capable+of+simulating+large-scale+wind-driven+circulation+and+thermohaline+circulation%2C+serving+as+the+oceanic+component+of+coupled+climate+models+%28referred+to+as+the+climate+ocean+model%29.+LICOM+is+based+on+the+primitive+equations%2C+continuity+equation%2C+equation+of+state+of+seawater%2C+and+conservation+equations+for+temperature+and+salinity.+Under+given+initial+and+boundary+conditions%2C+it+is+numerically+solved+using+the+finite+difference+method.+After+more+than+30+years+of+continuous+development%2C+LICOM+has+now+reached+its+third+version+%28LICOM3%29%2C+which+features+multiple+resolutions+and+parameterization+schemes.&collaborative_institutions=%22%22&established=None&family_type=%22component%22)

- [LICOM](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+LICOM&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22ocean%22&representative_member=LICOM3&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs00376-019-9208-5%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs13351-012-0305-y%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.ocemod.2022.102063%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs00376-020-0150-3%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs00376-020-0057-z&website=https%3A%2F%2Fearthlab-data.iap.ac.cn%2FLFS%2F&primary_institution=%22cas+the+institute+of+atmospheric+physics+%28iap%29%2C+chinese+academy+of+sciences+%28cas%29%22&description=The+LICOM+ocean+general+circulation+model+is+a+global+ocean+circulation+model+independently+developed+by+the+Ocean+Modelling+Group+at+the+Institute+of+Atmospheric+Physics+%28IAP%29%2C+Chinese+Academy+of+Sciences+%28CAS%29.+It+aims+to+provide+a+numerical+ocean+model+capable+of+simulating+large-scale+wind-driven+circulation+and+thermohaline+circulation%2C+serving+as+the+oceanic+component+of+coupled+climate+models+%28referred+to+as+the+climate+ocean+model%29.+LICOM+is+based+on+the+primitive+equations%2C+continuity+equation%2C+equation+of+state+of+seawater%2C+and+conservation+equations+for+temperature+and+salinity.+Under+given+initial+and+boundary+conditions%2C+it+is+numerically+solved+using+the+finite+difference+method.+After+more+than+30+years+of+continuous+development%2C+LICOM+has+now+reached+its+third+version+%28LICOM3%29%2C+which+features+multiple+resolutions+and+parameterization+schemes.&collaborative_institutions=%22%22&established=None&family_type=%22component%22)

- [M-SIM](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+M-SIM&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22sea_ice%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.accre.2021.07.003%3B%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-14-4891-2021%3B%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs00376-022-1316-y%3B%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Ftc-18-1215-2024%3B%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.3390%2Frs16091494%3B%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.ocemod.2025.102640&website=https%3A%2F%2Fprivate&primary_institution=%22sysu%22&description=M-SIM+%28Multiscale+Sea+Ice+Model%29+is+the+sea+ice+component+model.+It+includes+a+variety+of+improved+and+new+sea+ice+thermodynamic+and+dynamic+parameterizations+and+numerical+methods+that+are+suitable+to+simulate+multi-scale+changes+of+sea+ice+under+rapidly+changing+polar+climate.+The+code+base+follows+the+physics+common+to+the+CMIP7+family%2C+and+aligns+with+Earth+System+Models+standardized+by+the+World+Climate+Research+Programme.&collaborative_institutions=%22cas%22&established=2024&family_type=%22component%22)

- [MIROC](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+MIROC&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22atmosphere%22%2C%22land-surface%22%2C%22ocean%22%2C%22sea_ice%22&representative_member=none&references=&website=none&primary_institution=%22miroc%22&description=Model+for+Interdisciplinary+Research+on+Climate+-+jointly+developed+by+JAMSTEC%2C+AORI%2C+and+NIES.&collaborative_institutions=&established=none&family_type=%22model%22)

- [PISM](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+PISM&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22land-ice%22&representative_member=PISM+v2.1%2C+PISM+v2.3.1&references=https%3A%2F%2Fagupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com%2Fdoi%2F10.1029%2F2008JF001179%2C+https%3A%2F%2Ftc.copernicus.org%2Farticles%2F5%2F715%2F2011%2F%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fzenodo.org%2Frecords%2F19443904&website=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.pism.io%2F+https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fpism%2Fpism&primary_institution=%22uaf%22&description=The+Parallel+Ice+Sheet+Model+%28PISM%29+is+an+open+source%2C+parallel%2C+high-resolution+land+ice+%28i.e.+ice+sheet%29+model%2C+principally+driven+by+gravity+and+the+surface+mass+balance.+Gravity+is+partly+balanced+by+lateral+and+bottom+friction%2C+the+resulting+shear+stress+balance+in+the+ice+body+%28which+depends+on+the+ice+temperature+field+via+a+polythermal%2C+enthalpy-based+energy-conserving+scheme%29+determines+the+ice+flow.+PISM+includes+marine+ice+sheet+physics+with+dynamic+calving+fronts.&collaborative_institutions=%22pik%22&established=2006&family_type=%22component%22)

- [SI3](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+SI3&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22sea_ice%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5281%2Fzenodo.7534899&website=&primary_institution=%22nemo+consortium%22&description=SI3+%28Sea+Ice+modelling+Integrated+Initiative%29+is+the+sea+ice+engine+of+NEMO+%28Nucleus+for+European+Modelling+of+the+Ocean%29.+https%3A%2F%2Fzenodo.org%2Frecords%2F7534900+https%3A%2F%2Fnemo-ocean.eu%2F&collaborative_institutions=%22%22&established=&family_type=%22component%22)

- [access-esm](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+access-esm&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22aerosol%22%2C%22atmosphere%22%2C%22land-surface%22%2C%22ocean%22%2C%22ocean-biogeochemistry%22%2C%22sea_ice%22&representative_member=ACCESS-ESM1-5&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-10-2567-2017%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1071%2FES19035&website=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.access-nri.org.au%2Fmodels%2Fearth-system-model-esm%2F&primary_institution=%22csiro%22&description=The+Australian+Community+Climate+and+Earth+System+Simulator+%28ACCESS%29+is+a+group+of+model+versions+used+in+Australia+for+all+timescales+from+numerical+weather+prediction+to+centennial-scale+climate+simulations.+Component+models+used+in+ACCESS+vary+for+applications+at+different+timescales.+ACCESS-ESM+is+the+earth+system+model+version+used+for+centennial-scale+simulations%2C+including+an+interactive+carbon+cycle.+ACCESS-ESM+comprises+the+UK+Met+Office+Unified+Model+atmosphere%2C+the+CABLE+land+surface+model+including+land+biogeochemistry%2C+the+MOM+ocean+model+with+WOMBAT+for+ocean+biogeochemistry+and+the+CICE+sea-ice+model.+OASIS-MCT+has+been+used+for+coupling+but+ACCESS-ESM3+%28under+development%29+will+use+NUOPC.&collaborative_institutions=%22access-nri%22&established=2014&family_type=%22model%22)

- [awi-esm3](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+awi-esm3&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22atmosphere%22%2C%22land-ice%22%2C%22land-surface%22%2C%22ocean%22%2C%22ocean-biogeochemistry%22%2C%22sea_ice%22%2C%22aerosol%22&representative_member=AWI-ESM3-4-2&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-15-6399-2022&website=https%3A%2F%2Fawi-cm3-documentation.readthedocs.io%2Fen%2Flatest%2F&primary_institution=%22awi%22&description=Scientific+focus+and+approach++AWI-ESM3+is+the+Earth+System+Model+line+developed+at+the+Alfred+Wegener+Institute+around+a+globally+multi-resolution+unstructured-mesh+ocean+coupled+to+a+state-of-the-art+global+hydrostatic+atmosphere+with+interactive+vegetation.+The+premise+of+the+line+is+that+climate+fidelity+in+polar+regions+and+along+narrow+shelves+can+be+improved+at+controlled+global+cost+by+locally+refining+the+ocean+mesh+under+a+uniform+global+atmosphere%2C+rather+than+by+raising+resolution+everywhere.+AWI-ESM3+is+configured+for+historical+and+scenario+CMIP-class+simulations+and+for+paleo+and+pre-CMIP+transient+experiments.+Particular+attention+is+given+to+the+representation+of+Arctic+sea-ice+cover+and+drift%2C+sub-polar+gyre+variability%2C+the+Atlantic+Meridional+Overturning+Circulation%2C+and+Antarctic+ice-shelf+cavity+overturning%2C+all+of+which+the+underlying+components+have+been+developed+to+address.++Common+code+base+and+coupling+architecture++AWI-ESM3+members+are+built+from+a+fixed+component+stack.+OpenIFS+provides+the+atmospheric+component.+FESOM2+provides+the+ocean%2C+with+sea-ice+handled+internally+through+embedded+FESIM2.+LPJ-GUESS+provides+the+interactive+land+surface+and+vegetation.+Coupling+between+OpenIFS+and+FESOM2+is+handled+by+OASIS3-MCT+version+5%2C+with+conservative+remapping+weights+between+the+OpenIFS+reduced-Gaussian+grid+and+the+FESOM+unstructured+triangular+mesh+generated+offline+through+the+AWI+ocp-tool+workflow+and+the+YAC+remapping+libraries.+The+same+weights+deliver+atmospheric+forcing+onto+the+regular-Gaussian+equivalent+grid+on+which+LPJ-GUESS+is+integrated.+Atmosphere+to+ocean+coupling+exchanges+momentum%2C+freshwater+and+heat+fluxes+at+sub-daily+frequency%2C+with+sea+ice+treated+as+part+of+FESOM+and+not+as+a+separate+coupling+endpoint.+Relative+to+other+coupled+configurations+of+OpenIFS+and+FESOM2%2C+AWI-ESM3+adds+a+calving+and+iceberg+freshwater+channel%2C+an+enthalpy-of-fusion+correction+at+solid-runoff+entry%2C+and+a+prognostic+exchange+of+carbon%2C+water+and+energy+between+LPJ-GUESS+and+HTESSEL+through+OASIS+at+the+atmospheric+coupling+step.+River+runoff+is+routed+on+the+atmospheric+grid+before+delivery+to+FESOM+coastal+points.+Where+active%2C+ice-shelf+cavities+exchange+heat+and+freshwater+with+the+ocean+through+a+dedicated+cavity-flux+interface.+Build+and+configuration+of+the+coupled+executable+is+managed+through+the+AWI+fork+of+esm-tools%2C+allowing+reproducible+bit-identical+restarts+across+all+family+members.++How+family+members+differ++Members+of+AWI-ESM3+differ+principally+along+two+axes.+The+first+is+the+horizontal+and+vertical+resolution+of+the+atmosphere+and+ocean.+The+second+is+the+choice+of+cryosphere+and+biogeochemistry+options+inherited+from+the+underlying+FESOM2+and+FESIM2+components.++The+horizontal+resolution+of+the+atmosphere+is+set+by+the+truncation+of+the+OpenIFS+reduced-Gaussian+grid%2C+and+the+vertical+resolution+by+the+choice+of+OpenIFS+level+set.+The+horizontal+resolution+of+the+ocean+is+set+by+the+choice+of+FESOM+unstructured+triangular+mesh%2C+whose+nominal+resolution+and+pattern+of+regional+refinement+are+mesh-specific.+LPJ-GUESS+is+integrated+on+the+regular-Gaussian+equivalent+of+whichever+OpenIFS+truncation+is+used%2C+so+that+the+vegetation+grid+matches+the+native+atmospheric+resolution+in+each+member.+Standard-resolution+configurations+are+built+around+OpenIFS+at+TCO95+on+91+vertical+levels+paired+with+FESOM+meshes+whose+nominal+resolution+varies+between+about+20+and+120+km+globally+and+is+refined+in+the+polar+oceans+and+along+major+boundary+currents.+High-resolution+configurations+are+built+around+OpenIFS+at+TCO319+on+137+vertical+levels+paired+with+FESOM+meshes+whose+nominal+resolution+varies+between+about+5+and+25+km+globally+and+is+refined+in+the+Arctic+Ocean+and+in+the+Southern+Ocean%2C+with+additional+refinement+along+the+major+western+boundary+currents.++Beyond+resolution%2C+members+may+differ+in+cryosphere+and+biogeochemistry+options.+Activation+of+ice-shelf+cavities%2C+of+the+explicit+iceberg+drift+and+melt+module%2C+of+ocean+biogeochemistry+through+REcoM%2C+and+of+transient+passive+tracer+panels+for+CFCs%2C+SF6%2C+radiocarbon+and+water+isotopes+are+independent+compile-+and+run-time+options+that+may+differ+between+members.+Future+members+are+intended+to+host+an+interactive+land-ice+realm+through+two-way+coupling+with+the+Parallel+Ice+Sheet+Model+%28PISM%29%2C+and+to+expand+the+role+of+REcoM+as+a+routinely+active+ocean-biogeochemistry+component.+Neither+is+active+in+the+fast-track+CMIP7+submissions+but+both+fall+under+the+AWI-ESM3+family+for+later+contributions.&collaborative_institutions=%22ec-earth-consortium%22&established=2025&family_type=%22model%22)

- [cable](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+cable&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22land-surface%22&representative_member=CABLE2.4&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.4225%2F08%2F58615c6a9a51d&website=https%3A%2F%2Fdocs.access-hive.org.au%2Fmodels%2Fmodel_components%2Fland%2F&primary_institution=%22csiro%22&description=The+Community+Atmosphere+Biosphere+Land+Exchange+%28CABLE%29+model+is+a+land+surface+model+developed+in+Australia.+It+incorporates+land+biogeochemistry+using+the+CASA-CNP+module.+It+can+be+run+as+a+standalone+model+or+embedded+in+an+atmosphere+model.+Versions+of+CABLE+are+used+within+the+ACCESS+earth+system+model+and+within+the+Conformal+Cubic+Atmosphere+Model+%28CCAM%29.+One+version+of+the+standalone+model+includes+tree+demography+%28CABLE-POP%29+but+this+feature+is+not+yet+used+within+ACCESS.&collaborative_institutions=%22unsw%22%2C%22access-nri%22&established=2003&family_type=%22component%22)

- [cannemo](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+cannemo&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22ocean%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-12-4823-2019&website=&primary_institution=%22cccma%22&description=CanNEMO+was+introduced+in+CanESM5+%28Swart+et+al.%2C+2019%29+and+introduced+modifications+to+mesoscale+and+small-scale+mixing+physics+to+NEMO3.4.1.++https%3A%2F%2Fgitlab.com%2Fcccma%2Fcannemo&collaborative_institutions=%22%22&established=&family_type=%22component%22)

- [cas-fgoals-g3.5](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+cas-fgoals-g3.5&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22atmosphere%22%2C%22land-surface%22%2C%22ocean%22%2C%22sea_ice%22&representative_member=CAS-FGOALS-g3&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2019MS002012&website=https%3A%2F%2FNo+website&primary_institution=%22cas%22&description=The+four+component+models+of+FGOALS-g3.5+include+Version+3+of+the+Grid-Point+Atmospheric+Model+of+LASG-IAP+%28GAMIL3%29+for+the+atmosphere%2C+Version+3+of+the+LASG-IAP+Climate+System+Ocean+Model+%28LICOM3%29+for+the+ocean%2C+Version+4+of+the+Los+Alamos+sea+ice+model+for+sea+ice%2C+and+the+CAS-Land+Surface+Model+%28CAS-LSM%29+for+the+land.+In+LICOM3%2C+the+freshwater+exchange+process+between+river+model+and+the+ocean+model+employs+the+nearest+mapping+method+rather+than+the+area+conservative+mapping+method%2C+as+the+former+ensures+that+the+ocean+sufficiently+receives+the+riverine+freshwater+input.&collaborative_institutions=%22cas%22&established=2019&family_type=%22model%22)

- [class-ctem](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+class-ctem&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22land-surface%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-12-4823-2019%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-16-5427-2023&website=&primary_institution=%22cccma%22&description=CLASS+and+CTEM+simulate+the+physical+and+biogeochemical+land+surface+processes%2C+respectively%2C+and+together+they+calculate+fluxes+of+energy%2C+water%2C+CO2%2C+and+wetland+CH4+emissions+at+the+land%E2%80%93atmosphere+boundary.++https%3A%2F%2Fgitlab.com%2Fcccma%2Fcanam&collaborative_institutions=%22%22&established=&family_type=%22component%22)

- [cmoc](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+cmoc&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22ocean-biogeochemistry%22&references=Christian+et+al.+2022%3A%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-15-4393-2022+Swart+et+al.+2019%3A%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-12-4823-2019&website=&primary_institution=%22cccma%22&description=Canadian+Model+of+Ocean+Carbon+%28CMOC%29%3B+NPZD+ecosystem+with+OMIP+prescribed+carbonate+chemistry.++https%3A%2F%2Fgitlab.com%2Fcccma%2Fcannemo&collaborative_institutions=%22%22&established=&family_type=%22component%22)

- [ec-earth](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+ec-earth&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22atmosphere%22%2C%22ocean%22%2C%22sea_ice%22%2C%22ocean-biogeochemistry%22%2C%22land-surface%22%2C%22land-ice%22%2C%22atmospheric-chemistry%22%2C%22aerosol%22&representative_member=EC-Earth3&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-15-2973-2022%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1007%2Fs00382-011-1228-5&website=https%3A%2F%2Fec-earth.org%2F&primary_institution=%22ec-earth-consortium%22&description=Community-developed+Earth+system+model+based+on+model+components+like%3A++1.+The+ECMWF+Integrated+Forecasting+System+%28IFS%29+for+the+atmosphere+2.+The+Nucleus+for+European+Modelling+of+the+Ocean+%28NEMO%29+3.+A+dynamic+vegetation+model+%28LPJ-GUESS%29&collaborative_institutions=%22dmi%22%2C%22smhi%22%2C%22knmi%22%2C%22cnr%22%2C%22fmi%22%2C%22ichec%22&established=2006&family_type=%22model%22)

- [fesim](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+fesim&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22sea_ice%22&representative_member=FESIM+2.7&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-8-1747-2015&website=https%3A%2F%2Ffesom.de%2F&primary_institution=%22awi%22&description=1.+Scientific+focus+and+approach+The+Finite-Element+Sea+Ice+Model+%28FESIM%29%2C+developed+at+the+Alfred+Wegener+Institute%2C+is+a+sea-ice+model+formulated+on+unstructured+triangular+meshes+and+used+as+a+component+of+the+Finite-Element+Sea+ice+Ocean+Model+%28FESOM%29.+Its+scientific+focus+is+the+dynamics+and+thermodynamics+of+polar+sea+ice+on+multi-resolution+meshes+that+allow+regional+refinement+of+the+ice+cover%2C+marginal+ice+zones%2C+narrow+straits+and+ice-shelf+cavities+within+the+same+global+configuration+as+the+host+ocean.++2.+Common+code+base+and+physics+All+FESIM+family+members+share+a+common+Fortran+code+base+distributed+inside+the+FESOM+repository.+Common+elements+include+a+viscous-plastic+momentum+equation%2C+an+elastic-viscous-plastic+%28EVP%29+solver+and+a+modified+EVP+variant+with+improved+stability+compared+to+the+standard+EVP+approach%2C+a+flux-corrected+transport+algorithm+to+advect+the+ice+and+snow+mean+thicknesses+and+concentration%2C+and+a+thermodynamic+formulation+with+prognostic+ice+and+snow+thickness.+The+model+assumes+a+collocated+placement+of+ice+velocities%2C+mean+thicknesses+and+concentration+at+mesh+vertices%2C+and+relies+on+piecewise-linear+continuous+elements.++3.+How+family+members+differ+Family+members+differ+along+several+axes.+One+axis+is+the+underlying+code+generation%3A+FESIM-1%2C+the+original+sea-ice+component+of+FESOM+1.4+formulated+with+linear+finite+elements%2C+and+FESIM-2%2C+the+rewritten+formulation+used+inside+FESOM2+from+2017+onward.+Another+axis+is+the+rheology+configuration%2C+with+the+choice+between+the+standard+elastic-viscous-plastic+solver+and+the+modified+EVP+variant%2C+and+the+number+of+subcycling+iterations+selected+for+each.+A+further+axis+is+the+mesh+configuration%2C+with+FESIM+run+on+a+wide+range+of+FESOM+meshes+from+approximately+1%C2%B0+global+meshes+down+to+sub-kilometre+regional+meshes%2C+with+or+without+active+ice-shelf+cavities.&collaborative_institutions=%22%22&established=2003&family_type=%22component%22)

- [fesom](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+fesom&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22ocean%22&representative_member=FESOM+2.0&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-7-663-2014%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-10-765-2017%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-8-1747-2015%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-12-4875-2019%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-15-335-2022%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-12-3991-2019&website=https%3A%2F%2Ffesom2.readthedocs.io%2Fen%2Flatest%2F&primary_institution=%22awi%22&description=%2A%2AScientific+focus+and+approach%2A%2A+FESOM+%28Finite-Element%2FvolumE+Sea+ice%E2%80%93Ocean+Model%29%2C+developed+at+the+Alfred+Wegener+Institute+%28AWI%29%2C+is+a+global+sea-ice%2Focean+general+circulation+model+designed+around+a+single+guiding+idea%3A+the+use+of+unstructured+horizontal+meshes+to+enable+seamless+multi-resolution+simulation+of+the+global+ocean.+Rather+than+nesting+limited-area+models+inside+a+coarser+global+grid%2C+FESOM+allows+mesh+resolution+to+vary+smoothly+across+the+global+domain%2C+so+that+regions+of+dynamical+interest+%E2%80%94+western+boundary+currents%2C+the+Arctic+and+Southern+Oceans%2C+marginal+seas%2C+eddy-active+zones%2C+or+coastal+shelves+%E2%80%94+can+be+locally+refined+to+eddy-permitting+or+eddy-resolving+scales+while+retaining+a+coarser+grid+elsewhere.+This+makes+FESOM+particularly+suited+to+climate+applications+where+regional+process+detail+and+global+integrity+must+be+maintained+simultaneously%2C+and+it+has+become+a+widely+used+platform+for+exploring+the+benefits+of+unstructured-mesh+modelling+in+climate+research.++%2A%2ACommon+code+base+and+physics%2A%2A+All+FESOM+family+members+share+the+same+overarching+modelling+philosophy%3A+solution+of+the+hydrostatic+Boussinesq+primitive+equations+on+triangular+surface+meshes+with+a+vertical+discretisation+in+z-coordinates+%28with+optional+partial+cells%2C+sigma+layers+under+ice+shelves%2C+and+arbitrary+Lagrangian%E2%80%93Eulerian+%28ALE%29+layers+in+FESOM2%29.+The+sea-ice+component+is+the+dynamic%E2%80%93thermodynamic+Finite-Element+Sea+Ice+Model+%28FESIM%29%2C+based+on+an+elastic%E2%80%93viscous%E2%80%93plastic+%28EVP%2C+or+modified+mEVP%29+rheology%2C+with+optional+coupling+to+ICEPACK+column+physics+in+more+recent+configurations.+Common+physics+packages+include+the+K-Profile+Parameterization+%28KPP%29+or+TKE-based+vertical+mixing%2C+Gent%E2%80%93McWilliams+parameterization+of+mesoscale+eddy+transport+and+isoneutral+Redi+diffusion+at+non-eddying+resolutions%2C+and+standard+parameterizations+for+tidal+mixing+and+bottom+boundary+layer+flow.+Tracers+are+advected+using+flux-corrected+transport+%28FCT%29+schemes.+The+code+is+parallelised+with+MPI+and+%28in+FESOM2%29+supports+hybrid+CPU%2FGPU+execution%3B+unstructured-mesh+I%2FO+is+handled+either+directly+or+through+XIOS.++%2A%2AHow+family+members+differ%2A%2A+Family+members+differ+principally+in+numerical+core%2C+mesh%2C+and+complexity%3A++FESOM1.4+uses+a+continuous-Galerkin+finite-element+dynamical+core+and+is+documented+by+Wang+et+al.+%282014%29.+FESOM2+%28patch+versions+FESOM2.0%E2%80%93FESOM2.7%29+replaces+the+finite-element+core+with+a+finite-volume+formulation+on+cell-vertex+triangular+meshes%2C+adds+an+ALE+vertical+coordinate+framework%2C+and+delivers+roughly+a+3%C3%97+throughput+improvement+over+FESOM1.4+at+comparable+accuracy.+FESOM2+is+documented+by+Danilov+et+al.+%282017%29+and+further+described+by+Scholz+et+al.+%282019%2C+2022%29.+Mesh+and+resolution+is+the+most+visible+source+of+differentiation+between+configurations%3A+meshes+range+from+CMIP-class+low-resolution+%28~100%E2%80%93150+km+in+the+open+ocean+with+refinement+in+polar+and+dynamically+active+regions%2C+e.g.+the+CORE2+or+CMIP6+LR+mesh%29+through+medium-resolution+Arctic-focused+and+DYAMOND+meshes%2C+to+globally+eddy-resolving+meshes+at+1%2F10%C2%B0+%28~10+km%29+and+storm-resolving+meshes+down+to+~5+km+used+in+nextGEMS.+Sea-ice+complexity+varies+between+configurations%3A+standard+FESIM+zero-layer+or+single-category+thermodynamics+in+CMIP-class+runs%2C+versus+multi-category+ICEPACK+thermodynamics+in+other+setups.+Vertical+levels+and+mixing+choices+%28KPP+vs+TKE+via+CVMix%2C+with%2Fwithout+GM%2C+isoneutral+Redi+on%2Foff%29+are+tuned+per+configuration+depending+on+whether+the+mesh+resolves+mesoscale+eddies.&collaborative_institutions=%22%22&established=2004&family_type=%22component%22)

- [gelato](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+gelato&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22sea_ice%22&representative_member=none&references=&website=none&primary_institution=%22cnrm%22&description=GELATO+sea+ice+model+family+developed+by+CNRM%2FM%C3%A9t%C3%A9o-France.&collaborative_institutions=&established=none&family_type=%22component%22)

- [gfdl](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+gfdl&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22aerosol%22%2C%22atmosphere%22%2C%22atmospheric-chemistry%22%2C%22land-surface%22%2C%22ocean%22%2C%22ocean-biogeochemistry%22%2C%22sea_ice%22&representative_member=GFDL-ESM4.1&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2019MS002015&website=https%3A%2F%2Fagupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com%2Fdoi%2Ffull%2F10.1029%2F2019MS002015&primary_institution=%22noaa-gfdl%22&description=Coupled+models+using+the+GFDL+atmosphere%2C+land%2C+sea+ice%2C+and+ocean+components+making+use+of+the+Flexible+Modeling+System+coupling+infrastructure+starting+with+the+GFDL-CM2+series+%28Delworth+et+al.%2C+2006%29+in+CMIP3%2C+GFDL-CM3+and+GFDL-ESM2+series+in+CMIP5%2C+and+GFDL-ESM4.1+in+CMIP6.++Delworth%2C+T.+L.%2C+and+Coauthors%2C+2006%3A+GFDL%E2%80%99s+CM2+global+coupled+climate+models.+Part+I%3A+Formulation+and+simulation+characteristics.+J.+Climate%2C+19%2C+643%E2%80%93674.&collaborative_institutions=%22%22&established=2004&family_type=%22model%22)

- [ifs](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+ifs&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22atmosphere%22&representative_member=OpenIFS+48r1&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.21957%2Fslk503fs2i%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1175%2FJAS-D-13-0163.1%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1002%2Fqj.713%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1175%2F2008JHM1068.1%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2019MS001725%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1175%2FMWR-D-18-0043.1%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1256%2Fqj.03.173&website=https%3A%2F%2Fconfluence.ecmwf.int%2Fdisplay%2FOIFS&primary_institution=%22ecmwf%22&description=Family+description+The+Integrated+Forecasting+System+%28IFS%29+is+the+global+numerical+weather+prediction+and+atmospheric+modelling+system+developed+and+maintained+at+ECMWF.+The+IFS+family+encompasses+the+operational+forecasting+model+used+at+ECMWF+since+the+1980s%2C+its+data+assimilation+system%2C+and+the+portable+research+configuration+OpenIFS%2C+which+provides+the+full+IFS+forecast+capability+without+the+data+assimilation+system.+IFS+is+used+for+medium-range%2C+extended-range+and+seasonal+weather+forecasting%2C+atmospheric+reanalyses+%28ERA+family%29%2C+and+as+the+atmospheric+component+of+coupled+Earth+system+configurations+%28e.g.+EC-Earth%2C+AWI-CM-3%29.+Scientific+focus+and+approach+The+IFS+family+is+designed+around+a+global+hydrostatic+primitive-equation+atmospheric+model+that+prognoses+wind%2C+temperature%2C+specific+humidity%2C+cloud+condensate+%28liquid%2C+ice%29%2C+precipitation+species+%28rain%2C+snow%29%2C+cloud+fraction+and+surface+pressure+on+a+terrain-following+mass-based+vertical+coordinate.+The+IFS+simulates+the+global+three-dimensional+atmospheric+circulation+together+with+its+interactions+with+the+land+surface%2C+sea+ice+and+ocean+wave+fields%2C+and+%E2%80%94+in+more+recent+cycles+%E2%80%94+atmospheric+chemistry%2C+aerosol+and+prognostic+ozone.+Coupled+subsystems+include+the+HTESSEL+land-surface+scheme+%28soil+moisture+and+temperature%2C+vegetation%2C+snow%2C+lakes+via+FLake%29%2C+the+WAM+ocean+wave+model%2C+and+the+CAMS+chemistry+and+aerosol+package.+Common+code+base%2C+dynamical+core+and+physics+All+IFS+family+members+share+a+common+code+base+and+dynamical+core%3A+hydrostatic%2C+two-time-level%2C+semi-implicit+and+semi-Lagrangian%2C+using+spectral+transforms+between+a+reduced+Gaussian+grid+%28where+physics+and+advection+are+computed%29+and+spherical-harmonic+spectral+space+%28where+the+Helmholtz+problem+is+solved%29.+The+vertical+discretisation+uses+a+finite-element+scheme+%28Untch+%26+Hortal%2C+2004%3B+Vivoda+et+al.%2C+2018%29.+The+shared+physics+package+includes+the+turbulence+scheme+of+K%C3%B6hler+et+al.+%282011%29%2C+shallow+and+deep+convection+following+Bechtold+et+al.+%282014%29%2C+the+prognostic+cloud+scheme+%28Tiedtke+%2F+Forbes+%26+Tompkins+lineage%29%2C+microphysics%2C+the+ecRad+radiation+scheme%2C+and+the+HTESSEL+land+surface+%28Balsamo+et+al.%2C+2009%3B+Arduini+et+al.%2C+2019+for+the+multi-layer+snow+scheme%29.+Stochastic+physics+is+provided+through+the+SPPT+scheme.+How+family+members+differ+Family+members+differ+principally+in+cycle%2C+configuration+and+intended+use.+The+IFS+cycle+%28e.g.+38r1%2C+40r1%2C+41r2%2C+43r3%2C+47r3%2C+48r1%2C+49r1%29+determines+the+scientific+content+of+the+model%2C+with+each+cycle+bringing+updates+to+physics%2C+dynamics+and+coupling.+OpenIFS+releases+%2840r1%2C+43r3%2C+43r3v2%2C+48r1%29+provide+research-oriented+portable+counterparts+to+selected+operational+cycles%2C+scientifically+identical+to+the+forecast+model+of+the+corresponding+IFS+cycle+but+without+data+assimilation.+Configurations+also+differ+in+horizontal+resolution+%28e.g.+TCo399%2C+TCo1279%2C+TCo3999%29%2C+vertical+resolution+%28typically+91+or+137+levels%29%2C+inclusion+of+the+wave+model%2C+activation+of+CAMS+chemistry%2Faerosol%2C+and+choice+of+single-+or+double-precision+builds.&collaborative_institutions=%22m%C3%A9t%C3%A9o-france%22&established=1983&family_type=%22component%22)

- [lim](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+lim&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22sea_ice%22&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F97JC00480%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.ocemod.2009.01.004%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.ocemod.2008.10.005&website=&primary_institution=%22uclouvain%22&description=LIM+is+a+numerical+model+of+sea+ice+designed+for+climate+studies+and+operational+oceanography.+It+is+coupled+to+the+ocean+general+circulation+model+OPA+%28Oc%C3%A9an+Parall%C3%A9lis%C3%A9%29+and+is+part+of+NEMO+%28Nucleus+for+European+Modeling+of+the+Ocean%29.+LIM+is+used+in+several+global+climate+models+contributing+to+the+assessment+reports+of+the+IPCC+%28Intergovernmental+Panel+on+Climate+Change%29.+The+code+is+freely+available+under+a+software+licensing+agreement+and+can+be+downloaded+from+the+NEMO+website+%28www.nemo-ocean.eu%2F%29.++Source%3A+https%3A%2F%2Fwww.cmcc.it%2Fmodels%2Flim&collaborative_institutions=%22%22&established=&family_type=%22component%22)

- [lpj-guess](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+lpj-guess&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22land-surface%22&representative_member=LPJ-GUESS+4.1&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1046%2Fj.1466-822X.2001.t01-1-00256.x%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1046%2Fj.1365-2486.2003.00569.x%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1016%2Fj.jhydrol.2003.09.029%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fbg-11-2027-2014%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fesd-4-385-2013%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fbg-12-2489-2015%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.1029%2F2008GB003412%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-14-6071-2021%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-15-6709-2022&website=https%3A%2F%2Fweb.nateko.lu.se%2Flpj-guess%2F&primary_institution=%22kit%22&description=%2A%2AScientific+focus+and+approach%2A%2A+LPJ-GUESS+%28Lund%E2%80%93Potsdam%E2%80%93Jena+General+Ecosystem+Simulator%29%2C+developed+primarily+at+Lund+University%2C+is+a+process-based+dynamic+global+vegetation+model+%28DGVM%29+that+simulates+the+structure%2C+composition%2C+growth+and+biogeochemistry+of+terrestrial+ecosystems+under+varying+climate%2C+atmospheric+CO%E2%82%82+and+land-use+forcing.+Its+scientific+focus+is+the+coupled+carbon%E2%80%93nitrogen%E2%80%93water+cycling+of+the+land+biosphere%2C+with+a+strong+emphasis+on+ecological+realism%3A+rather+than+treating+each+plant+functional+type+as+a+single+grid-cell+average%2C+LPJ-GUESS+represents+vegetation+as+a+population+of+age-+and+size-structured+cohorts+of+woody+individuals+competing+for+light%2C+water+and+nutrients+within+multiple+replicate+%22patches%22+per+grid+cell%2C+with+an+herbaceous+understorey+and+stochastic+disturbance+and+mortality.+This+individual-+and+patch-based+design+%E2%80%94+inherited+from+forest+gap+models+%E2%80%94+allows+the+model+to+capture+demographic+processes%2C+stand+development%2C+succession+and+the+response+of+community+composition+to+climatic%2C+edaphic+and+disturbance+gradients%2C+alongside+the+standard+DGVM+outputs+of+net+primary+production%2C+net+ecosystem+exchange%2C+runoff%2C+vegetation+carbon+and+soil+carbon+and+nitrogen+pools.++%2A%2ACommon+code+base+and+physics%2A%2A+All+LPJ-GUESS+family+members+share+a+common+C%2B%2B+code+base+and+a+core+set+of+process+representations+originally+derived+from+the+LPJ-DGVM+%28Sitch+et+al.%2C+2003%29+and+extended+in+Smith+et+al.+%282001%2C+2014%29.+Common+elements+across+the+family+include%3A+a+coupled+photosynthesis%E2%80%93stomatal-conductance+scheme+based+on+the+Farquhar%E2%80%93Collatz+formulation+%28Haxeltine+%26+Prentice+1996%29%3B+a+growing-degree-day+phenology+with+chilling+and+water-stress+controls%3B+allometric+tree+growth+and+tissue+turnover%3B+an+autotrophic+and+heterotrophic+respiration+scheme+with+prognostic+litter+and+soil+organic+matter+pools+%28CENTURY-style+after+the+v3.0+update%29%3B+a+soil+hydrology+scheme+with+two+physical+layers+in+the+standard+formulation+%28Gerten+et+al.%2C+2004%29+extended+to+a+multi-layer+permafrost-capable+scheme+in+the+arctic+configurations%3B+a+coupled+terrestrial+nitrogen+cycle+%28from+v3.0+onward%3B+Smith+et+al.%2C+2014%29+including+biological+N+fixation%2C+deposition%2C+leaching+and+plant%E2%80%93microbial+competition%3B+representations+of+natural+disturbance+through+fire+%28BLAZE%2C+GlobFIRM+or+SIMFIRE-BLAZE+depending+on+configuration%29+and+stand-replacing+disturbance%3B+and+a+managed-land+module+%28from+v4.0%3B+Lindeskog+et+al.%2C+2013%29+supporting+croplands+with+major+C3%2FC4+crop+functional+types%2C+irrigation%2C+fertilisation+and+rotation%2C+plus+pasture+and+managed+forestry.+Simulations+are+typically+run+as+ensembles+of+replicate+patches+%28commonly+~25+per+grid+cell%29+on+grids+of+0.5%C2%B0+resolution+or+finer%2C+driven+by+climate%2C+CO%E2%82%82%2C+N+deposition+and+land-use+forcing.++%2A%2AHow+family+members+differ%2A%2A+Family+members+differ+principally+in+process+complexity%2C+target+ecosystems%2C+and+coupling+architecture%3A++-+LPJ-GUESS+v2.x+%28Smith+et+al.+2001%3B+Ahlstr%C3%B6m+et+al.+2012%3B+Hickler+et+al.+2012%29+is+the+original+carbon-and-water-only+version+with+global+or+European+plant-functional-type+sets%2C+used+in+many+CMIP5-era+applications.+-+LPJ-GUESS+v3.0+%28Smith+et+al.+2014%29+introduced+the+coupled+C%E2%80%93N+cycle+with+N+limitation+on+photosynthesis+and+decomposition%2C+and+remains+the+scientific+reference+for+most+current+applications.+-+LPJ-GUESS+v4.0%2B+%28Lindeskog+et+al.+2013%3B+Olin+et+al.+2015%3B+Lindeskog+et+al.+2021%29+adds+the+managed-land+module+%28crops%2C+pasture%2C+managed+forests%29.+-+LPJ-GUESS+arctic+%2F+WHyMe+%2F+peatland+configurations+%28Wania+et+al.+2009%3B+McGuire+et+al.%2C+Chadburn+et+al.%2C+Pongracz+et+al.+2021%29+add+multi-layer+soil+thermodynamics%2C+freeze%E2%80%93thaw%2C+soil+organic+layers%2C+peatland+hydrology%2C+methane+production%2Foxidation+and+a+multi-layer+snow+scheme+for+high-latitude+applications.+-+LPJ-GUESS%2FLSM+v1.0+%28Mart%C3%ADn+Belda+et+al.+2022%29+is+a+next-generation+reformulation+as+a+full+land-surface+model+with+a+closed+surface+energy+balance%2C+prognostic+canopy+and+soil+temperatures%2C+and+sub-daily+physics%2C+designed+for+two-way+coupling+with+atmospheric+models.+-+Process+extensions+developed+across+the+family+include+alternative+fire+schemes+%28SIMFIRE-BLAZE%2C+LMfire-CF%29%2C+BVOC+emissions%2C+lightning-induced+mortality%2C+hydraulic+mortality%2C+stable-water-isotope+tracers%2C+and+coupling+to+surface%E2%80%93subsurface+hydrology+models+%28e.g.+ParFlow-LPJG%29.+-+PFT+sets+and+parameterisations+also+differ+between+configurations%3A+a+standard+global+PFT+set+%28~11+PFTs%29%2C+a+more+detailed+European+PFT+set%2C+region-specific+sets+for+the+tropics%2C+boreal+North+America+and+the+Greater+Yellowstone+Ecosystem%2C+and+crop-functional-type+sets+in+the+managed-land+variants.&collaborative_institutions=%22pik%22&established=2001&family_type=%22component%22)

- [wombat](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=model_family.yml&title=Modify%3A+Model+Family%3A+wombat&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&scientific_domains=%22ocean-biogeochemistry%22&representative_member=WOMBATlite&references=https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fbg-22-5349-2025%2C+https%3A%2F%2Fdoi.org%2F10.5194%2Fgmd-6-591-2013&website=https%3A%2F%2Fwombat-docs.readthedocs.io%2Fstable%2F&primary_institution=%22csiro%22&description=The+World+Ocean+Model+of+Biogeochemistry+And+Trophic-dynamics+%28WOMBAT%29+is+the+ocean+biogeochemistry+module+added+to+ACCESS+models+that+use+MOM+%28Modular+Ocean+Model%29.+The+core+of+WOMBAT+is+a+Nutrient+-+Phytoplankton+-+Zooplankton+-+Detritus+%28NPZD%29+framework.+The+original+WOMBAT+version+was+described+in+Oke+et+al.+%282013%29.+Two+current+WOMBAT+models+exist%3A+WOMBAT-lite+and+WOMBAT-mid.+WOMBAT-lite+is+designed+to+be+a+simple+and+general+biogeochemical+model+of+the+global+ocean+that+is+computationally+efficient.+WOMBAT-mid+is+designed+to+be+a+more+complex+and+realistic+ocean+biogeochemical+model+that+is+more+computationally+costly+but+provides+a+greater+resolution+of+ecosystem+and+biogeochemical+processes.+A+more+complete+description+of+both+models+can+be+found+at+https%3A%2F%2Fwombat-docs.readthedocs.io%2Fstable%2F&collaborative_institutions=%22access-nri%22&established=2013&family_type=%22component%22)

- [v135](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=vertical_computational_grid.yml&title=Modify%3A+Vertical+Computational+Grid%3A+v135&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&bottom_layer_thickness=36.6667&total_thickness=4000&n_z_range=&n_z=31&description=The+vertical+grid+of+a+classic+land+ice+sheet+model+typically+consists+of+a+fixed+number+of+layers+which+are+scaled+for+each+horizontal+grid+point+individually+per+time+step+matching+the+total+ice+thickness+at+this+horizontal+point.+Near+the+bottom+of+the+ice+body+often+the+layers+are+relative+closer+to+each+other+than+at+the+ice+sheet+surface.+So+the+layer+thickness+varies+for+each+horizontal+point+and+per+layer%2C+where+the+vertical+coordinate+is+always+zero+at+the+surface+and+one+at+the+bottom+of+the+ice+body.&top_layer_thickness=230&vertical_coordinate=%22height%22)

- [v146](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=vertical_computational_grid.yml&title=Modify%3A+Vertical+Computational+Grid%3A+v146&issue_kind=%22Modify%22&bottom_layer_thickness=&total_thickness=9341&n_z_range=&n_z=72&description=The+ocean+vertical+coordinate+is+a+hybrid+between+fixed+pressure+levels+towards+the+upper+boundary+and+potential+density%2C+with+reference+pressure+at+2000+dbar%2C+below.+Using+high-order+vertical+reconstructions+of+potential+temperature+and+salinity%2C+the+vertical+position+of+a+layer+interface+is+continuously+nudged+to+minimise+the+difference+between+the+interface+and+its+associated+reference+potential+density.+Thereafter%2C+a+prescribed+minimum+pressure+thickness+per+layer+is+enforced+towards+the+upper+boundary.+The+interface+reference+potential+densities+are+allowed+to+slowly+vary+in+time+to+optimise+the+global+potential+density+range+as+the+model+state+evolves.+Since+the+model+layers+have+spatially+and+temporally+varying+thickness+at+depth%2C+a+fixed+bottom+layer+thickness+is+not+specified.&top_layer_thickness=2&vertical_coordinate=%22ocean-hybrid-pressure-isopycnic-coordinate%22)
